content:
# 大纲规划任务

项目仓库: sgl-project/sglang
项目名: sglang-python-sglang
讲义目录: sglang-python-sglang-tutorial/
模式: incremental
当前 HEAD: 59ef3b15cc86eb64c48cd5e687a95dbefb872a29
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/59ef3b15cc86eb64c48cd5e687a95dbefb872a29/python/sglang/
用户关注点: （无）

上次 HEAD: d0b9689805232d8ab37789121cbc3b766b5c723e

## 现有大纲 manifest

```json
{
  "project": "sglang-python-sglang",
  "head": "d0b9689805232d8ab37789121cbc3b766b5c723e",
  "rationale": "SGLang 是一个体量极大的高性能 LLM/多模态推理服务框架，代码分为前端语言层（python/sglang/lang）与运行时层（python/sglang/srt）两大部分，srt 下又有 40+ 子系统。因此学习路线采用「先宏观后微观、先运行后原理、先单机后分布式」的九单元渐进结构：第 1 单元让读者从零认识项目并跑起服务、看清目录；第 2 单元讲前端 DSL 与执行机制，它是理解「请求从哪里来」的入口；第 3 单元讲服务端进程拓扑与配置/上下文/环境变量三大基础设施；第 4 单元深入调度核心与 TokenizerManager→Scheduler→DetokenizerManager 的请求主链路，这是整个框架的心脏；第 5 单元从 Scheduler 下沉到 ModelRunner/ForwardBatch/注意力后端的 GPU 执行；第 6 单元专讲 SGLang 的招牌特性 RadixAttention 与 KV 缓存内存池及其多种变体；第 7 单元覆盖 TP/PP/DP/EP 等大规模并行；第 8 单元讲 PD 分离、投机解码、LoRA、结构化输出、多模态、采样等高级特性；第 9 单元面向二次开发（新增模型、量化、硬件后端、编译、可观测性、测试）。单元之间设了明确的 depends_on，使读者始终能找到前置讲义；每篇讲义只解决一个主题、引用 1-6 个真实源码文件、并配一个可操作的代码实践任务。由于项目庞大，共规划 46 篇讲义，符合大型项目 30+ 的规模要求。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "入门与项目定位",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "SGLang 是什么：项目定位与架构总览",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 SGLang 的定位（高性能 LLM/多模态推理服务框架）、核心特性（RadixAttention、零开销调度、分离式部署、投机解码、结构化输出等）以及前端 lang 与运行时 srt 的两层架构。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 SGLang 解决什么问题、和 vLLM/TensorRT-LLM 的差异",
            "记住前端语言层（lang）与运行时层（srt）的职责划分",
            "了解 SGLang 支持的模型、硬件与典型应用场景（服务 + RL rollout）"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "__init__.py",
            "version.py",
            "global_config.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "sglang 顶层包导出的公共 API",
            "global_config 默认配置"
          ],
          "practice_task": "安装 sglang（pip install \"sglang[all]\"），打印 sglang.__version__，并用 sglang.Engine 跑一句 \"Hello\" 生成，确认环境可用。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "从零启动：安装与运行第一个推理服务",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-install-and-run-server.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 sglang 的 CLI 入口（serve/generate/version）、launch_server.run_server 的分发逻辑，以及如何用 sglang serve 启动一个可接收请求的 HTTP 服务。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 sglang serve 的命令行用法与参数传递",
            "理解 launch_server.run_server 如何根据 server_args 选择 HTTP/Ray/gRPC 模式",
            "能够独立启动服务并用 curl 验证"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "cli/main.py",
            "cli/serve.py",
            "launch_server.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "cli 子命令分发",
            "run_server 模式分发",
            "prepare_server_arg"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用一个小模型（如 Qwen2.5-0.5B）启动 sglang serve，记录启动日志中的端口与 worker 数量，并用 curl 调用 /v1/models 确认服务在线。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "目录结构与代码地图：lang 与 srt",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-codebase-map.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "带读者走读 python/sglang 的目录结构，区分前端 lang/、运行时 srt/、cli/、kernels/、test/ 等顶层目录的职责，建立后续阅读的地图。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "记住 lang/、srt/、cli/、kernels/、test/ 各目录的职责",
            "了解 srt 下的关键子系统（entrypoints、managers、model_executor、mem_cache、layers、distributed 等）",
            "知道在哪个目录找哪类问题"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "__init__.py",
            "srt/server_args.py",
            "lang/api.py",
            "srt/entrypoints/http_server.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "顶层包结构",
            "srt 子系统概览"
          ],
          "practice_task": "绘制一份 sglang 目录思维导图，标注每个 srt 子目录（managers、model_executor、mem_cache、layers、distributed、speculative、disaggregation、lora、constrained）对应的一句话职责，并在源码中各找一个代表文件。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "发送请求：OpenAI 兼容 API 与 Engine 嵌入式 API",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-request-entrypoints.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "对比两种发请求的方式：通过 HTTP 的 OpenAI 兼容接口，以及通过同进程的 sglang.Engine 嵌入式 API，并说明二者如何进入运行时。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "会用 OpenAI SDK / curl 调用 /v1/chat/completions、/v1/completions、/v1/embeddings",
            "理解 sglang.Engine 作为同进程入口的优势（无需起 HTTP）",
            "看清 HTTP 请求与 Engine.generate 如何汇聚到运行时"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "srt/entrypoints/engine.py",
            "srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py",
            "srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_chat.py",
            "lang/api.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Engine 类",
            "OpenAI 协议与 serving_chat",
            "Runtime/Engine 前端工厂"
          ],
          "practice_task": "分别用 (a) OpenAI Python SDK 连本地 sglang serve、(b) 直接 sglang.Engine 两种方式跑同一段 chat 请求，对比返回结构，并打印两者的吞吐差异。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "前端语言层 lang/",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "前端 DSL 基础：function、gen、select、image",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-frontend-dsl-basics.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 SGLang 前端领域特定语言的核心原语：@function 定义可复用程序、gen 生成、select 在候选项中挑选、image/video 注入多模态输入。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "会用 @function 编写可复用的生成程序",
            "理解 gen/select 返回的是 SglExpr 表达式而非立即执行",
            "掌握多模态 image/video 原语的基本用法"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "lang/api.py",
            "lang/ir.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "gen/gen_int/gen_string",
            "select 与 choices",
            "function 装饰器",
            "SglExpr 表达式"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 @function 写一个三选一的分类程序：给定一个问题，用 select 在 ['A','B','C'] 中挑出答案，并打印 s.function 返回的对象类型，体会惰性求值。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l4"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "IR、Tracer 与解释器：前端程序如何执行",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-ir-tracer-interpreter.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "深入前端执行机制：tracer 把 @function 追踪成 IR（中间表示），interpreter 调度后端逐条解释执行这些 SglExpr。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 SGLang 前端「先追踪成 IR，再解释执行」的两阶段模型",
            "区分 tracer（静态收集）与 interpreter（动态执行）的职责",
            "能看懂 ir.py 中的 SglFunction/SglGen/SglSelect 数据结构"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "lang/ir.py",
            "lang/tracer.py",
            "lang/interpreter.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "SglFunction 与作用域",
            "tracer 追踪流程",
            "interpreter 调度 backend"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 interpreter 中加一行 print，观察一个带 gen 和 select 的 @function 被解释执行时，每条 SglExpr 的求值顺序，并用文字描述追踪-解释两阶段。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "后端抽象：RuntimeEndpoint 与第三方后端",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-backend-abstraction.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解前端如何通过统一的后端抽象对接不同推理提供方：自研 RuntimeEndpoint、OpenAI、Anthropic、LiteLLM 等，以及 set_default_backend 的作用。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 BaseBackend 抽象与 generate/stream 接口约定",
            "掌握 RuntimeEndpoint 如何把前端请求转发到 SGLang 运行时",
            "会切换默认后端（自研运行时 vs OpenAI 兼容服务）"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "lang/backend/base_backend.py",
            "lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py",
            "lang/backend/openai.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "BaseBackend 接口",
            "RuntimeEndpoint",
            "OpenAI 后端",
            "set_default_backend"
          ],
          "practice_task": "写一个 @function，先用 RuntimeEndpoint（连本地 sglang serve）跑一遍，再用 OpenAI 后端连同一个服务跑一遍，对比两者在前端层的行为差异。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l4",
          "title": "Chat 模板与 choices 选择策略",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u2-l4-chat-template-choices.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解前端如何用 chat_template 构造对话、system/user/assistant 角色原语，以及 select 的 choices_method（如 token_length_normalized）如何决定候选项胜出。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 system/user/assistant 角色原语与 chat 模板渲染",
            "掌握 choices 采样方法对 select 结果的影响",
            "能区分 greedy/likelihood 归一化策略"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "lang/chat_template.py",
            "lang/choices.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "角色原语",
            "chat_template 渲染",
            "ChoicesSamplingMethod",
            "token_length_normalized"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 user()/assistant() 原语构造一段多轮对话，并对同一个 select 分别用 token_length_normalized 和 unconditional_likelihood_normalized 两种 choices_method，观察结果差异并解释原因。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "服务端架构与请求生命周期",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "服务启动全流程：CLI 到 HTTP server",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-server-launch-flow.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "从 sglang serve 一路追到 http_server.launch_server，看清参数解析、插件加载、子进程拉起与 FastAPI 启动的完整链路。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 prepare_server_args 的参数解析与校验",
            "理解 launch_server 拉起子进程并组装引擎的步骤",
            "看清 _setup_and_run_http_server 如何挂载路由并启动 uvicorn"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "cli/serve.py",
            "launch_server.py",
            "srt/entrypoints/http_server.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "prepare_server_args",
            "load_plugins",
            "launch_server",
            "_setup_and_run_http_server"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 launch_server 关键步骤（拉起子进程、挂载路由、启动 uvicorn）前后加日志，启动一次服务，按时间顺序记录每个阶段，画出启动时序图。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "进程拓扑：三大管理器与 IPC 概览",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u3-l2-process-topology.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲清 SGLang 运行时的进程拓扑：HTTP/Engine/TokenizerManager 在主进程，Scheduler 与 DetokenizerManager 各为子进程，进程间用 ZMQ + io_struct 通信。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "记住三大管理器（TokenizerManager/Scheduler/DetokenizerManager）的进程归属与职责",
            "理解请求 token 流：Tokenizer→Scheduler→GPU→Detokenizer→Tokenizer",
            "了解 Engine._launch_subprocesses 如何编排这些进程"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "srt/entrypoints/engine.py",
            "srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py",
            "srt/managers/scheduler.py",
            "srt/managers/detokenizer_manager.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "TokenizerManager",
            "Scheduler",
            "DetokenizerManager",
            "Engine._launch_subprocesses"
          ],
          "practice_task": "启动服务后用 ps/jstack 或日志确认主进程与子进程数量，标注每个进程对应的类，画出一次请求在这几个进程间的流转箭头图。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l3",
          "title": "ServerArgs 配置体系",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u3-l3-server-args.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 ServerArgs 这个超大数据类如何承载所有服务参数、prepare_server_args 如何解析命令行，以及 arg_groups 如何按特性分组管理参数。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 ServerArgs 作为「运行时单一配置源」的设计",
            "掌握 prepare_server_args 的解析、默认值与校验逻辑",
            "了解 arg_groups 按特性（PD/投机/HiSparse 等）分组扩展参数的方式"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "srt/server_args.py",
            "srt/server_args_config_parser.py",
            "srt/arg_groups/arg_utils.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "ServerArgs 数据类",
            "prepare_server_args",
            "arg_groups 分组钩子"
          ],
          "practice_task": "挑三个常用参数（如 --tp、--mem-fraction-static、--context-length），在 server_args.py 中找到定义与默认值，启动服务时分别修改并观察行为变化。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l4",
          "title": "RuntimeContext：运行时上下文与资源租约",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u3-l4-runtime-context.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 RuntimeContext 如何组织运行时配置与进程级状态（分层上下文、resolve-at-end 的 ServerArgs、资源/stream/buffer 租约、每 forward 标志）。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 RuntimeContext 的分层结构与 resolve-at-end 设计",
            "掌握资源租约（stream/buffer）与每 forward 标志的概念",
            "知道在哪里读运行时配置、避免使用模块级可变状态"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "srt/runtime_context.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "RuntimeContext 分层",
            "ServerArgs override 入口",
            "资源/stream/buffer 租约"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 runtime_context.py 中找出「resolve-at-end」相关的字段与 override 入口，写一段说明：为什么不在模块顶层缓存运行时配置，而要经过 RuntimeContext。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l5",
          "title": "环境变量约定与 environ",
          "order": 5,
          "filename": "u3-l5-environ-vars.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 srt/environ.py 如何集中定义所有 SGLANG_* 环境变量、命名/访问/弃用约定，以及它们如何影响运行时行为。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 SGLANG_* 环境变量的定义与访问方式",
            "理解 SGL_* 旧别名迁移与新变量命名约定",
            "学会用环境变量做轻量调参与灰度开关"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "srt/environ.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "environ 集中定义",
            "SGLANG_* 命名约定",
            "别名迁移"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 environ.py 中选一个影响性能的环境变量（如控制 CUDA graph 或调度行为的），设与不设各跑一次 bench_one_batch.py，对比吞吐/延迟差异并记录。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u4",
      "title": "调度核心与 Manager 管道",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 4,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u4-l1",
          "title": "io_struct 与进程间通信（ZMQ + msgspec）",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u4-l1-io-struct-ipc.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 io_struct 如何用 msgspec.Struct 定义跨进程消息（GenerateReqInput/BatchStrOutput 等），以及基于 ZMQ 的读写管道如何在这些进程间传递。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 io_struct 中 Req/Output 消息的 msgspec 建模",
            "掌握 ZMQ 管道在 Tokenizer/Scheduler/Detokenizer 间的角色",
            "能看懂一次请求的消息收发时序"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "srt/managers/io_struct.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "BaseReq/BaseBatchReq",
            "GenerateReqInput",
            "BatchStrOutput",
            "ZMQ 读写管道"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 io_struct.py 中列出 GenerateReqInput 的关键字段，并画一张图说明一条请求从 TokenizerManager 到 Scheduler 再回到 DetokenizerManager 各用了哪些消息类型。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u3-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u4-l2",
          "title": "TokenizerManager：请求接入与 token 化",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u4-l2-tokenizer-manager.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 TokenizerManager 如何接收 HTTP/Engine 请求、做 token 化与多模态预处理、分配 request id，并把请求投递给 Scheduler。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 TokenizerManager 的异步事件循环与请求接入",
            "掌握 token 化、多模态输入处理与请求对象构造",
            "看清 TokenizerManager 与 Scheduler 的收发关系"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py",
            "srt/managers/tokenizer_manager_score_mixin.py"
```

```json
  "rationale": "SGLang 是一个体量极大的高性能 LLM/多模态推理服务框架，代码分为前端语言层（python/sglang/lang）与运行时层（python/sglang/srt）两大部分，srt 下又有 40+ 子系统。因此学习路线采用「先宏观后微观、先运行后原理、先单机后分布式」的九单元渐进结构：第 1 单元让读者从零认识项目并跑起服务、看清目录；第 2 单元讲前端 DSL 与执行机制，它是理解「请求从哪里来」的入口；第 3 单元讲服务端进程拓扑与配置/上下文/环境变量三大基础设施；第 4 单元深入调度核心与 TokenizerManager→Scheduler→DetokenizerManager 的请求主链路，这是整个框架的心脏；第 5 单元从 Scheduler 下沉到 ModelRunner/ForwardBatch/注意力后端的 GPU 执行；第 6 单元专讲 SGLang 的招牌特性 RadixAttention 与 KV 缓存内存池及其多种变体；第 7 单元覆盖 TP/PP/DP/EP 等大规模并行；第 8 单元讲 PD 分离、投机解码、LoRA、结构化输出、多模态、采样等高级特性；第 9 单元面向二次开发（新增模型、量化、硬件后端、编译、可观测性、测试）。单元之间设了明确的 depends_on，使读者始终能找到前置讲义；每篇讲义只解决一个主题、引用 1-6 个真实源码文件、并配一个可操作的代码实践任务。由于项目庞大，共规划 46 篇讲义，符合大型项目 30+ 的规模要求。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "入门与项目定位",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {

          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 SGLang 的定位（高性能 LLM/多模态推理服务框架）、核心特性（RadixAttention、零开销调度、分离式部署、投机解码、结构化输出等）以及前端 lang 与运行时 srt 的两层架构。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 SGLang 解决什么问题、和 vLLM/TensorRT-LLM 的差异",
            "记住前端语言层（lang）与运行时层（srt）的职责划分",
            "了解 SGLang 支持的模型、硬件与典型应用场景（服务 + RL rollout）"

          "source_files": [
```

## 执行要求

请按照 planner prompt 中的规则执行。

你需要输出：

- 一个完整 manifest JSON。
- full 模式下，所有讲义 `action` 为 `"new"`。
- incremental 模式下，根据 diff 判断每篇讲义的 `action`。

不要写任何 Markdown 讲义文件。

不要输出 JSON 以外的任何文字。

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- add-jit-kernel: Step-by-step tutorial for adding a new lightweight JIT CUDA kernel to sglang's jit_kernel module
- add-sgl-kernel: Step-by-step tutorial for adding a heavyweight AOT CUDA/C++ kernel to sgl-kernel (including tests & benchmarks)
- ci-workflow-guide: Guide to SGLang CI workflow orchestration — stage ordering, fast-fail, gating, partitioning, execution modes, and debugging CI failures. Use when modifying CI workflows, adding stages, debugging CI pipeline issues, or understanding how tests are dispatched and gated across stages.
- cookbook-migrate-model: Migrate a legacy-template SGLang cookbook page (monolithic per-model generator under docs_new/src/snippets/autoregressive/) onto the config-driven template (shared _deployment.jsx / _playground.jsx engines + per-model config). Use when asked to migrate, convert, or port an existing cookbook page — NOT for brand-new models (use cookbook-add-model for those). Run with /cookbook-migrate-model <Model page name, e.g. GLM-5.1>.
- cookbook-review-pr: Review a pull request against the SGLang Cookbook (docs_new/, Mintlify) contribution checklist — the config-driven format (per-model config + benchmarks JSX consumed by the shared _deployment.jsx / _playground.jsx engines). Run with /cookbook-review-pr <PR number>.
- debug-cuda-crash: Call this skill when you need to debug CUDA crashes in SGLang using kernel API logging
- debug-distributed-hang: Debug hanging issues in SGLang distributed inference (TP/PP/DP/EP). Covers identifying hang locations via py-spy/watchdog/cuda coredump, per-rank logging to find state divergence, binary-search methodology for locating the first diverge point, and fix patterns. Use when a multi-GPU SGLang run hangs, freezes, or times out during collective operations.
- env-var-conventions: Conventions for SGLang environment variables — where to define, how to access, how to name, and how to deprecate. Use when adding, renaming, or reviewing any `SGLANG_*` environment variable (or migrating a legacy `SGL_*` alias), or when touching `python/sglang/srt/environ.py`.
- generate-profile: Generate an e2e profiling trace of an SGLang server run. Launches a server, validates accuracy, captures a Chrome-compatible trace, and returns the profile path.
- large-class-style: Code style for SGLang large classes `Scheduler`, `TokenizerManager`, and `ModelRunner`: frozen-code conventions and `__init__` orchestration style. Use when modifying any of these three classes or reviewing changes to them.
- llm-torch-profiler-analysis: Unified LLM torch-profiler triage skill for `sglang`, `vllm`, `TensorRT-LLM`, and `TokenSpeed`. Use it to inspect an existing `trace.json(.gz)` or profile directory, or to drive live profiling against a running server when supported and return one three-table report with kernel, overlap-opportunity, and fuse-pattern tables.
- mechanical-refactor-verify: Make mechanical refactoring (file splits, function moves, module extractions, renames) machine-checkable instead of eyeballed. Reproduce a relocation commit byte-for-byte from faithful primitives, and split an extraction into a verifiable prepare + move + postpare. Use when doing or reviewing such changes.
- scripted-runtime-notes: Requirements for the SGLang scripted runtime, chiefly when to add (vs not add) a harness API. Use for anything related to the scripted runtime.
- sglang-bisect-ci-regression: Investigate consistently failing SGLang CI tests by extracting the failure signature from scheduled or rerun workflows, bisecting the passing/failing commit window, checking runner or hardware specificity, and optionally reproducing on a remote GPU host.
- sglang-cherrypick: Trigger the bot-cherry-pick workflow for a batch of merged PRs onto a release branch and monitor each run to completion. Use when an SGLang release manager asks to cherry-pick a list of PRs to a release branch.
- sglang-prod-incident-triage: Replay-first debug flow for SGLang serving problems. Use when a live or recent server shows health-check failures, latency or throughput regressions, queue growth, timeouts, distributed stalls, crash dumps, wrong outputs after deploys, or PD/EP/HiCache issues, and the job is to turn the problem into a replay plus the right next debug tool.
- sglang-runtime-context: How SGLang's runtime configuration and process-global state are organized (RuntimeContext tiers, resolve-at-end ServerArgs, override entry point, resource/stream/buffer leases, per-forward flags), the CI guardrails that enforce the design, and the idioms for developing and testing against it. Load this before touching server_args, model overrides, module-level state, or per-forward state in sglang.
- speculative-naming: Naming conventions for SGLang speculative decoding identifiers. Use when adding, renaming, or reviewing identifiers in speculative decoding code — anything under `python/sglang/srt/speculative/`, related attention backends, scheduler accumulators, IPC fields, observability metrics, or CLI flags.
- write-sglang-test: Guide for writing SGLang CI/UT tests. Covers CustomTestCase, CI registration, server fixtures, model selection, mock testing, and test placement. Always read test/README.md for the full CI layout, how to run tests, and extra tips. Use when creating new tests, adding CI test cases, writing unit tests, or when the user asks to add tests for SGLang features.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Now, the server_args.py change introduces `enable_cp_decode_attn_tp` which is a CP-related param. This reinforces that CP is an active feature area.

Let me now verify:

"""Context parallel strategy abstractions."""

from sglang.srt.layers.cp.base import (
    BaseContextParallelMetadata,
    ContextParallelStrategy,
    ContextParallelStrategyKind,
    CPAttentionBackendKind,
    get_cp_strategy,
    get_cp_strategy_kind,
    init_cp_strategy,
    is_cp_enabled,
    is_interleave,
    is_zigzag,
)

    InterleaveContextParallelMetadata,
    InterleaveCPStrategy,
)
from sglang.srt.layers.cp.zigzag import (
    ContextParallelMetadata,
    ZigzagContextParallelMetadata,
    ZigzagCPStrategy,
)

__all__ = [
    "BaseContextParallelMetadata",
    "CPAttentionBackendKind",
    "ContextParallelMetadata",
    "ContextParallelStrategy",
    "ContextParallelStrategyKind",
    "InterleaveCPStrategy",
    "InterleaveContextParallelMetadata",
    "ZigzagCPStrategy",
    "ZigzagContextParallelMetadata",
    "get_cp_strategy",
    "get_cp_strategy_kind",

    "is_cp_enabled",
    "is_interleave",
    "is_zigzag",
]

content.content:
1	# Copyright 2023-2026 SGLang Team
2	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
3	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
4	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
5	#
6	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
7	#
8	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
9	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
10	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
11	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
12	# limitations under the License.
13	# ==============================================================================
14	
15	"""Base types and process-wide helpers for context parallel strategies.
16	
17	The strategy implementation is split across:
18	
19	* ``base.py``: base ABC, base metadata dataclass, enums, and singleton helpers.
20	* ``zigzag.py``: former in-seq-split strategy and zigzag metadata.
21	* ``interleave.py``: former round-robin-split strategy and interleave metadata.
22	* ``utils.py``: public re-exports for import convenience.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from enum import IntEnum
30	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Callable, List, Optional, Tuple
31	
32	from sglang.srt.runtime_context import get_parallel
33	
34	if TYPE_CHECKING:
35	    from sglang.srt.model_executor.forward_batch_info import ForwardBatch
36	    from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
37	
38	
39	class ContextParallelStrategyKind(IntEnum):
40	    """Context parallel strategy identifiers."""
41	
42	    NONE = 0
43	    ZIGZAG = 1
44	    INTERLEAVE = 2
45	
46	    @classmethod
47	    def from_string(cls, value: str) -> ContextParallelStrategyKind:
48	        if value == "zigzag":
49	            return cls.ZIGZAG
50	        if value == "interleave":
51	            return cls.INTERLEAVE
52	        raise ValueError(
53	            f"Unknown cp_strategy={value!r}; expected one of "
54	            "{'zigzag', 'interleave'}"
55	        )
56	
57	    @property
58	    def cli_value(self) -> str:
59	        return {
60	            ContextParallelStrategyKind.NONE: "none",
61	            ContextParallelStrategyKind.ZIGZAG: "zigzag",
62	            ContextParallelStrategyKind.INTERLEAVE: "interleave",
63	        }[self]
64	
65	
66	class CPAttentionBackendKind(IntEnum):
67	    """Attention backend calling convention used by CP strategy dispatch."""
68	
69	    FLASH_ATTENTION = 0
70	    TRTLLM_MHA = 1
71	
72	    @classmethod
73	    def from_string(cls, value: str) -> CPAttentionBackendKind:
74	        if value in ("fa3", "fa4", "flashinfer"):
75	            return cls.FLASH_ATTENTION
76	        if value == "trtllm_mha":
77	            return cls.TRTLLM_MHA
78	        raise ValueError(
79	            f"Unsupported attention_backend={value!r} for CP strategy; expected one "
80	            "of {'fa3', 'fa4', 'flashinfer', 'trtllm_mha'}"
81	        )
82	
83	
84	@dataclass
85	class BaseContextParallelMetadata:
86	    total_seq_lens: int = 0
87	    bs: int = 1
88	
89	
90	class ContextParallelStrategy(ABC):
91	    """Owns process-wide policy for one context parallel layout."""
92	
93	    name: str
94	    kind: ContextParallelStrategyKind
95	
96	    def __init__(self, cp_size: int):
97	        self.cp_size = cp_size
98	
99	    @property
100	    def cp_rank(self) -> int:
101	
102	        return get_parallel().attn_cp_rank
103	
104	    @property
105	    def per_layer_attn_cp_comm(self) -> bool:
106	        return _is_dsa_active()
107	
108	    @abstractmethod
109	    def can_apply(self, num_tokens: int, forward_batch: ForwardBatch) -> bool:
110	        """Return True if this strategy can shard the current forward."""
111	
112	    @abstractmethod
113	    def build_metadata(
114	        self,
115	        num_tokens: int,
116	        seqs_len: Optional[List[int]],
117	        extend_seqs_len: Optional[List[int]] = None,
118	    ) -> BaseContextParallelMetadata:
119	        """Build per-forward metadata for this strategy."""
120	
121	    @abstractmethod
122	    def shard_hidden_states(self, x: Any, forward_batch: ForwardBatch) -> Any:
123	        """Shard hidden states to the current CP rank, usually at the first layer."""
124	
125	    @abstractmethod
126	    def shard_position_ids(self, positions: Any, forward_batch: ForwardBatch) -> Any:
127	        """Shard KV-cache slot position IDs for each token to the current CP rank."""
128	
129	    @abstractmethod
130	    def gather_hidden_states(
131	        self,
132	        x: Any,
133	        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
134	        stream: Optional[Any] = None,
135	    ) -> Any:
136	        """Gather rank-local hidden states, usually at the last layer."""
137	
138	    @abstractmethod
139	    def gather_kv_cache(
140	        self,
141	        x: Any,
142	        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
143	        stream: Optional[Any] = None,
144	    ) -> Any:
145	        """Gather rank-local KV payloads back to full token order."""
146	
147	    def shard_per_request(
148	        self,
149	        extend_seqs_cpu: List[int],
150	        extend_seqs: Any,
151	    ) -> Tuple[List[int], Any, List[int], Any]:
152	        raise NotImplementedError(
153	            f"{self.name} strategy does not support per-request sharding"
154	        )
155	
156	    def split_before_forward(
157	        self,
158	        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
159	        input_ids: Optional[Any],
160	        positions: Any,
161	        input_embeds: Optional[Any] = None,
162	    ) -> Optional[Any]:
163	        """Shard model inputs before model.forward in CP-v2 paths."""
164	        if input_ids is not None:
165	            forward_batch.cp_v2_input_ids = self.shard_hidden_states(
166	                input_ids, forward_batch
167	            )
168	        forward_batch.positions = self.shard_position_ids(positions, forward_batch)
169	        if input_embeds is not None:
170	            return self.shard_hidden_states(input_embeds, forward_batch)
171	        return None
172	
173	    @abstractmethod
174	    def run_attention(
175	        self,
176	        q: Any,
177	        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
178	        device: Any,
179	        attn_fn: Callable[[Any, Any, Any, int], Any],
180	        attention_backend: CPAttentionBackendKind = CPAttentionBackendKind.FLASH_ATTENTION,
181	    ) -> Any:
182	        """Dispatch CP attention using the selected backend convention."""
183	
184	    @abstractmethod
185	    def materialize_full_kv(
186	        self,
187	        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
188	        layer: Any,
189	        k: Any,
190	        v: Any,
191	        swa_loc: Optional[Any] = None,
192	    ) -> None:
193	        """Write full-layout K/V to the backend cache if needed."""
194	
195	    def reindex_attn_metadata(self, core_attn_metadata: Any) -> None:
196	        """Optional attention metadata rewrite for strategies that need it."""
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	def _is_dsa_active() -> bool:
201	    from sglang.srt.runtime_context import get_server_args
202	
203	    sa = get_server_args()
204	    return bool(
205	        getattr(sa, "enable_prefill_cp", False)
206	        and getattr(sa, "_is_dsa_model_arch", False)
207	    )
208	
209	
210	_STRATEGY: Optional[ContextParallelStrategy] = None
211	
212	
213	def init_cp_strategy(server_args: ServerArgs) -> None:
214	    """Bind the configured CP strategy for this process."""
215	    global _STRATEGY
216	
217	    if not getattr(server_args, "enable_prefill_cp", False):
218	        _STRATEGY = None
219	        return
220	
221	    cp_size = getattr(server_args, "attn_cp_size", 1)
222	    if cp_size <= 1:
223	        _STRATEGY = None
224	        return
225	
226	    kind = ContextParallelStrategyKind.from_string(server_args.cp_strategy)
227	    if kind == ContextParallelStrategyKind.ZIGZAG:
228	        from sglang.srt.layers.cp.zigzag import ZigzagCPStrategy
229	
230	        _STRATEGY = ZigzagCPStrategy(cp_size=cp_size)
231	    elif kind == ContextParallelStrategyKind.INTERLEAVE:
232	        from sglang.srt.layers.cp.interleave import InterleaveCPStrategy
233	
234	        _STRATEGY = InterleaveCPStrategy(cp_size=cp_size)
235	    else:
236	        raise ValueError(
237	            f"Unsupported cp_strategy kind {kind} for "
238	            f"cp_strategy={server_args.cp_strategy!r}"
239	        )
240	
241	
242	def get_cp_strategy() -> Optional[ContextParallelStrategy]:
243	    """Return the configured strategy, initializing lazily on first call.
244	
245	    Subprocesses re-import this module with ``_STRATEGY = None`` and never
246	    re-run ``ServerArgs.__post_init__`` because the pickled instance bypasses
247	    ``__init__``. Lazy init lets worker processes recover the singleton from
248	    global server args.
249	    """
250	    global _STRATEGY
251	
252	    if _STRATEGY is None:
253	        from sglang.srt.runtime_context import get_server_args
254	
255	        try:
256	            server_args = get_server_args()
257	        except ValueError:
258	            return None
259	        if server_args is not None and getattr(server_args, "enable_prefill_cp", False):
260	            init_cp_strategy(server_args)
261	    return _STRATEGY
262	
263	
264	def get_cp_strategy_kind() -> ContextParallelStrategyKind:
265	    strategy = get_cp_strategy()
266	    if strategy is None:
267	        return ContextParallelStrategyKind.NONE
268	    return strategy.kind
269	
270	
271	def is_cp_enabled() -> bool:
272	    return get_cp_strategy() is not None
273	
274	
275	def is_zigzag() -> bool:
276	    return get_cp_strategy_kind() == ContextParallelStrategyKind.ZIGZAG
277	
278	
279	def is_interleave() -> bool:
280	    return get_cp_strategy_kind() == ContextParallelStrategyKind.INTERLEAVE
281

# Don't use `getattr` / `hasattr` for defensive access

Over-defensive `getattr(obj, "field", default)` / `hasattr(obj, "field")` hide
errors and defeat strict type checking. If a field is always present, accessing
it defensively is confusing and masks real bugs. Prefer:

1. **`isinstance` for type narrowing** — check the type, then access fields directly:

   ```python
   if (
       isinstance(obj, (TokenizedGenerateReqInput, TokenizedEmbeddingReqInput))
       and obj.mm_inputs
   ):
   ```
   (see `python/sglang/srt/managers/mm_utils.py`)

2. **Always set the field (to `None` if needed), then do a `None` check** — the
   field should always exist, so a `None` / non-`None` check is enough:

   ```python
   obj.field = None   # in __init__ / construction
   ...
   if obj.field is not None:
       ...
   ```

Bad — `server_args` always has `revision`, so `getattr` is misleading and swallows
a real `AttributeError` if the field is ever renamed:

```python
revision=getattr(server_args, "revision", None),   # BAD
revision=server_args.revision,                     # GOOD
```
(see `python/sglang/srt/managers/template_detection.py`)
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "**/*.py"
---

# Don't use `getattr` / `hasattr` for defensive access

Over-defensive `getattr(obj, "field", default)` / `hasattr(obj, "field")` hide
errors and defeat strict type checking. If a field is always present, accessing
it defensively is confusing and masks real bugs. Prefer:

1. **`isinstance` for type narrowing** — check the type, then access fields directly:

   ```python

       isinstance(obj, (TokenizedGenerateReqInput, TokenizedEmbeddingReqInput))
       and obj.mm_inputs
   ):
   ```
   (see `python/sglang/srt/managers/mm_utils.py`)

2. **Always set the field (to `None` if needed), then do a `None` check** — the
   field should always exist, so a `None` / non-`None` check is enough:

   ```python

   ...
   if obj.field is not None:
       ...
   ```

Bad — `server_args` always has `revision`, so `getattr` is misleading and swallows
a real `AttributeError` if the field is ever renamed:


# `ForwardBatch.init_new` must not mutate the ScheduleBatch

`init_new` (and any ForwardBatch factory) treats the input `ScheduleBatch` as
read-only. Per-forward overrides go through the kw-only params of `init_new` /
`TpModelWorker.forward_batch_generation`, never a ScheduleBatch field. Batch-prep
writes (`out_cache_loc`, `seq_lens_*`, ...) before `init_new` are out of scope.

Tolerated exceptions (don't add new ones):

- `seq_lens_sum` backfill — the `seq_lens` family is slated for removal (→ kv-committed lengths).
- `sampling_info` sub-object writes (grammars, canary ids) — shared object, until the sampling forward-copy op.
- `_expand_mrope_from_input` memoizing `mrope_position_delta_repeated_cache` — pre-existing.

# General Code Style

Default conventions for new and modified Python code. Prefer these unless there is a concrete reason not to; call out deviations in review.

- **Prefer stateless.** Favor pure functions over methods that mutate instance state; pass inputs in, return outputs out.
- **Prefer immutable.** Default to immutable data (frozen structs, tuples, read-only values); mutate only when there is a clear need.
- **Extract init-static values at construction.** When a derived value's inputs are frozen for the object's lifetime (typically configuration: constructor args, env vars, server args), compute it once in `__init__` and store it as a well-named attribute (`self.mtp_enabled`, `self.needs_cpu_seq_lens`); later code reads the attribute instead of re-deriving it. Input immutability is the hard precondition — if inputs can change, recompute in place or funnel mutation through a single override point (the frozen `ServerArgs.override()` pattern). If you can't give the value a meaningful name, the boundary is wrong — don't cache unnameable subexpressions.
- **Functions stay small.** Keep each function under ~100 LOC; split larger ones into named helpers.
- **Files stay small.** Keep each file under ~2k LOC; split larger modules along cohesive boundaries.
- **Core functions read like pseudocode.** The main / orchestration function of a unit should be short and read like algorithm pseudocode — push detail into well-named helpers so the top-level flow is obvious.
- **Avoid mixins.** Don't add behavior via mixin classes; prefer explicit composition (hold a collaborator and call it) or plain functions.
- **Prefer protected over public.** Default methods to protected (`_name`); expose only what callers actually use.
- **Prefer keyword arguments.** Call functions of 2+ args by keyword, and design APIs to be called that way.
- **Pass what you need, not the god object.** Give a callee the specific values it uses (by keyword), not a whole large object (`ModelRunner`, `Scheduler`); reserve passing the whole object for a leaf whose contract genuinely requires it. Even then, keep it read-only — read fields off it and return results for the caller to assign, rather than writing fields back through it.

  - "**/*.py"
---

- **Extract init-static values at construction.** When a derived value's inputs are frozen for the object's lifetime (typically configuration: constructor args, env vars, server args), compute it once in `__init__` and store it as a well-named attribute (`self.mtp_enabled`, `self.needs_cpu_seq_lens`); later code reads the attribute instead of re-deriving it. Input immutability is the hard precondition — if inputs can change, recompute in place or funnel mutation through a single override point (the frozen `ServerArgs.override()` pattern). If you can't give the value a meaningful name, the boundary is wrong — don't cache unnameable subexpressions.
- **Functions stay small.** Keep each function under ~100 LOC; split larger ones into named helpers.
- **Files stay small.** Keep each file under ~2k LOC; split larger modules along cohesive boundaries.

- **Prefer protected over public.** Default methods to protected (`_name`); expose only what callers actually use.
- **Prefer keyword arguments.** Call functions of 2+ args by keyword, and design APIs to be called that way.
- **Pass what you need, not the god object.** Give a callee the specific values it uses (by keyword), not a whole large object (`ModelRunner`, `Scheduler`); reserve passing the whole object for a leaf whose contract genuinely requires it. # Use `msgspec.Struct`, not `@dataclass`

Define data containers as `msgspec.Struct`. Do not add new
`dataclasses.dataclass` (or `attrs`) — they weaken strict type checking and
don't translate cleanly for multi-language support (e.g. the planned Rust migration).

```python
import msgspec

class LoadSnapshot(msgspec.Struct):   # prefer frozen= and omit_defaults=; kw_only= as needed
    dp_rank: int = 0
    tokens: list[int] = []            # mutable defaults are safe
```

- Methods / `@classmethod` constructors go on the `Struct`; see
  `python/sglang/srt/managers/load_snapshot.py`.
- New code only. Existing `@dataclass` is grandfathered — migrate opportunistically
  while editing the file, not in drive-by sweeps.

content.content:
# Mutate ScheduleBatch fields out of place

Never mutate a `ScheduleBatch` direct field in place — no `.extend()`/`.append()`,
`+=`/`-=`/`|=`, tensor `.add_()`/`.fill_()`, or slice/index assignment. Build the
new value and rebind the field:

```python
# Bad
self.reqs.extend(other.reqs)
self.seq_lens.add_(1)
self.extend_lens[i] -= encoder_len

# Good
self.reqs = self.reqs + other.reqs
self.seq_lens = self.seq_lens + 1
lens = self.extend_lens[:]; lens[i] -= encoder_len; self.extend_lens = lens  # loop a copy, rebind once
```

Why: `copy()` snapshots and the overlap scheduler's queued references rely on old
objects staying frozen (the prerequisite for the per-step immutable ScheduleBatch
refactor); derived values like `seq_lens_sum` only recompute safely when fields
are rebound.

Out of scope (mutation intended): penalizer buffers, CUDA graph static buffers,
`ForwardBatch` fields, attention-backend metadata.
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "python/sglang/srt/**/*.py"
---

# Mutate ScheduleBatch fields out of place

Never mutate a `ScheduleBatch` direct field in place — no `.extend()`/`.append()`,
`+=`/`-=`/`|=`, tensor `.add_()`/`.fill_()`, or slice/index assignment. Build the
new value and rebind the field:

```python
# Bad
self.reqs.extend(other.reqs)
self.seq_lens.add_(1)
self.extend_lens[i] -= encoder_len

# Good
self.reqs = self.reqs + other.reqs
self.seq_lens = self.seq_lens + 1
lens = self.extend_lens[:]; lens[i] -= encoder_len; self.extend_lens = lens  # loop a copy, rebind once
```

Why: `copy()` snapshots and the overlap scheduler's queued references rely on old
objects staying frozen (the prerequisite for the per-step immutable ScheduleBatch
refactor); derived values like `seq_lens_sum` only recompute safely when fields
are rebound.

Out of scope (mutation intended): penalizer buffers, CUDA graph static buffers,
`ForwardBatch` fields, attention-backend metadata.
